title: Education & Lifelong Learning
subtitle: Essential Vocabulary & Grammar for Aptis B2

sections.title: Education & Lifelong Learning
sections.quote: Essential Vocabulary & Grammar for Aptis B2

features.title: Advanced Vocabulary
features.description: Master Level B2 lexical sets focused on academic environments.

features.title: Paraphrasing
features.description: Developing synonyms and structural variety for higher scores.

features.title: Hypothetical Situations
features.description: Conditional structures for discussing educational choices.

features.title: Aptis Practice Tasks
features.description: Direct application to examination formats and criteria.

badge.title: APTIS ALIGNED
badge.text: This module focuses on B2 academic vocabulary, grammar control, and formal writing tasks.

tip.title: B2 Proficiency Tip
tip.text: For the Aptis B2 level, examiners look for collocations and idiomatic expressions related to learning. Instead of just saying "learn something," try "acquire a new skill" or "pursue academic excellence."

sections.title: 1. Advanced Vocabulary
sections.description: Tap cards to flip and listen.

cards.definition: Ex: Psychology is a complex academic discipline involving the study of the mind.
cards.translation: Một ngành học / môn học thuật

cards.definition: Ex: Distance learning has become essential for working professionals worldwide.
Essential for maintaining high standards in the corporate sector.
cards.translation: Phát triển chuyên môn liên tục

cards.definition: Ex: Workshops are a great way to acquire practical industry skills.
cards.translation: Tiếp thu kiến thức / kỹ năng

cards.definition: Ex: Students must enroll in the course before the semester begins.
Ex: Does he meet the requirements for this senior management position?

tip.title: Pro-Tip: Academic Verbs
tip.text: When discussing your career path, use "acquire" instead of "get" for a more professional tone.

sections.title: 2. Paraphrasing (Synonyms)
sections.description: Click B2 words to hear pronunciation and compare formal alternatives.

practiceTasks.label: Formal vs. Informal
practiceTasks.text: "I'm taking a class" -> "I have enrolled in a course"

practiceTasks.label: Formal vs. Informal
practiceTasks.text: "I'm learning new stuff" -> "I am acquiring professional skills"

tip.title: Pro-Tip: Precision Matters
tip.text: While these words are synonyms, they carry different nuances. Use "Crucial" for high-stakes situations, and "Essential" for necessary components of a process.

sections.title: 3. Hypothetical Situations
sections.description: Mastering the nuances of imaginary scenarios and expressing desires within professional academic contexts.

points.title: Conditionals Type 2
points.function: If + S + V-past, S + would/could + V-inf
examples: If I had more spare time, I would enroll in an advanced data science course.

points.title: Wish structures
examples: I wish our university offered more hands-on workshops for students.

rows: If I had more time, I would enroll.
Regret or desire about the current state
I wish the course offered more workshops.

tip.title: Academic Pro-Tip
tip.text: In formal writing, we often use "were" instead of "was" for all subjects in the if clause.

sections.title: Education Grammar Selection
sections.description: Refine grammatical precision and advanced writing skills.

questions.prompt: "If the government ________ more in vocational training, the skills gap would narrow significantly."
"I wish I ________ more opportunities to acquire specialized knowledge during my undergraduate years."

tip.title: Sentence Upgrade
tip.text: Original: "The course is very difficult. I want more teachers to help me." Upgrade: "This academic discipline is exceptionally challenging, and it would be highly beneficial if more tutors were available to support the learners."

for learners to stay self-motivated to meet all the academic

tip.title: Pro-Tip
tip.text: Look for collocations. The word "learning" is often paired with "distance" or "blended" in an academic context.

sections.title: Education Practice: Vocabulary
sections.description: Choose the most appropriate academic term to complete the sentences.

questions.prompt: "Biology is a demanding academic _________ that requires a lot of research."
"To be accepted into the Master's program, candidates must _________ all the entry requirements."
"Many professionals choose _________ learning because they can study while working full-time."
"Internships provide an excellent opportunity for students to _________ practical skills before graduating."
"Make sure to _________ in the advanced grammar course before the registration deadline."
"The company highly values employees who demonstrate a commitment to continuous professional _________."

tip.title: Academic Tip
tip.text: In academic English, "discipline" is often used as a more formal synonym for "field of study" or "subject."

sections.title: Reading Part 2
sections.description: Fill in the blanks with the correct words: acquire | continuous | discipline | enrolled | essential | meet | recognized

for success. The program helps students

professional development is vital in today's fast-paced economy. Therefore, I also plan to pursue a more advanced academic

tip.title: Pro-Tip
tip.text: Look for collocations and context clues. Pay attention to prepositions like "in" or "by" which often follow specific verbs.

sections.title: Education Practice: Writing Part 4
sections.description: Write a short formal response using the vocabulary learned.

prompt.situation: You are attending an English course for Master's preparation. However, the study materials are outdated. Write a short message (30-50 words) to the center using "I wish..." or "If I were..." and the vocabulary learned.
prompt.referenceAnswer: I wish the study materials were more up to date. If I were responsible for the course, I would provide more academic resources and practical guidance to help learners acquire the skills they need.

tip.title: B2 Writing Tip
tip.text: To achieve higher marks in the Writing component, elevate your tone by using sophisticated adverbs to qualify your adjectives, such as highly beneficial or extremely challenging.
